experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a systematic observation of luminous spots on a piece of meat, including measurements of size, shape, and location. The author mentions specific procedures (e.g., drawing the spinal medulla, examining tendons and fleshy parts) and attempts to quantify luminosity by comparing it to glowworms and reading letters on paper. This aligns with the definition of an experiment as a deliberate procedure to investigate a phenomenon.
experiments.description: The author examines a piece of meat to identify and analyze luminous spots. The experiment involves visually inspecting the meat, measuring the size and shape of these spots, and attempting to quantify their luminosity by applying flexible paper to the brightest areas to read letters. The author also notes the color of the light (greenish-blue) and the locations of these spots, including bones, tendons, and fleshy parts.
experiments.quote: In this one piece of meat I reckoned distinctly above twenty several places that did all of them shine, though not all of them alike, some of them doing it but very faintly. [...] And I perceived one place in a Tendon to afford some light; and lastly three or four spots in the fleshy parts at a good distance from the bones were plainly discovered by their own light, though that were fainter than in the parts above mentioned.
experiments.outcome: The author observed multiple luminous spots on the meat, described their varying sizes, shapes, and locations, and attempted to quantify their luminosity by reading letters on paper. The color of the light was noted as greenish-blue.
experiments.location: Null (the experiment appears to be conducted in a laboratory or controlled setting at the author's location, but no specific location is mentioned.)
